fact: May 10 is exactly 130 days into the year - that's more than a third of the whole year already gone!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is typically prime time for baby birds to hatch - their tiny peeping sounds fill the air as they crack out of their shells!
Dandelions are usually going crazy right now, turning from bright yellow flowers into those fluffy white puffballs you can blow wishes on.
The sun is climbing higher in the sky each day, making shadows shorter and giving you about 14 hours of daylight in most northern places.
If you're a Taurus, May 10 falls right in the middle of your zodiac season - you're ruled by Venus and supposedly love fancy food and cozy blankets.
In Australia and other southern places, trees are typically dropping their leaves like crazy right now as autumn gets serious.
Bees are usually working overtime collecting nectar from all the spring flowers - a single bee might visit 5,000 flowers in just one day!
The morning dew is often getting lighter as the air warms up, so spider webs covered in tiny water droplets become less common.
We're still about 6 weeks away from the summer solstice, but the days are getting longer by about 2-3 minutes every single day.
In many places, this is when you start seeing those annoying little gnats and flies buzzing around - they love the warm, humid spring air.
Grass grows so fast in mid-May that you can literally watch it get taller - it can grow up to 6 inches in a single week!
